denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We have reviewed the request for additional Pelvic floor physical therapy (12 visits) for the treatment of Pelvic floor dysfunction (N81.9). Based on Cigna Coverage Policy, this request is denied. The duration of Pelvic floor physical therapy exceeds plan limits without new clinical review. Continuation requires updated documentation demonstrating significant functional improvement, which was not received.

This review was conducted by Dr. A Smith.

Additional Plan Information:
This decision is based on a thorough review of the medical records submitted by your provider and the specific terms, conditions, limitations, and exclusions of your group health benefit plan. Under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) and other applicable laws, you are entitled to receive, upon request and free of charge, reasonable access to, and copies of, all documents, records, and other information relevant to your claim for benefits. This includes any clinical guidelines, medical policies, or algorithms used in making this adverse benefit determination.

If you disagree with this decision, you or your authorized representative may file a formal written appeal. Appeals must be submitted within 180 days of receiving this notice. Please include your member ID, reference number, and any additional clinical documentation, peer-reviewed medical literature, or physician letters of support that you believe may overturn this denial. Our appeals department will ensure that your case is reviewed by a health care professional who was not involved in the initial decision and who holds appropriate credentials in the relevant medical specialty. 

Furthermore, if your condition is deemed urgent—meaning that applying the standard timeframe for making a determination could seriously jeopardize your life, health, or ability to regain maximum function—you or your physician may request an expedited (urgent) appeal. Expedited appeals are typically resolved within 72 hours of receipt.
clinical_context: 58-year-old female with chronic Pelvic floor dysfunction (N81.9) manifesting as severe stress urinary incontinence and levator avulsion confirmed on ultrasound. She has completed an initial 12 sessions of pelvic floor physical therapy with slow but objective improvement in pelvic floor muscle strength (from 1/5 to 2/5 on Oxford scale) and reduced pad usage. An additional 12 visits of Pelvic floor physical therapy are medically necessary to reach functional goals. Stopping therapy now risks regression of the partial improvements achieved. The submitted clinical documentation clearly outlines functional progress and the need for continued skilled therapy.

guideline_applicability.reasoning: duration_excessive applies well to requesting visits beyond the cap.

flaw_injection_letter.reasoning: The letter names 'Dr. A Smith' without credentials and lacks any financial liability language.

tone_and_style.reasoning: The letter is appropriately formal and standard.
